title: First Response

jokes.text: She's kind of a lightweight now... She's a dud.
jokes.setup: Dan reassuring Selina that Janet needs this interview more than they do
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing the interviewer as a 'dud' while preparing for her show
jokes.explanation: Sharp character work showing the team's casual cruelty and overconfidence before things go wrong

jokes.text: You're a fraud and a liar, ma'am. Wow. What? What are you doing? I'm hitting you.
jokes.setup: Dan aggressively prepping Selina for tough interview questions
jokes.punchline: Selina's confused reaction to Dan literally 'hitting' her with hostile questions
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy mixed with the absurdity of aggressive prep for a 'puff piece'

jokes.text: It's conservative and desperate at the same time.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what Janet is wearing
jokes.punchline: Perfectly capturing a specific political aesthetic in four words
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - captures an entire political wardrobe strategy in one oxymoronic phrase

jokes.text: There's the bluff puff which we're gonna start out with... Then we switch right over to rough puff. Nobody does rough puff like you. Rrr. Give me that rough puff.
jokes.setup: Janet explaining her interview strategy using 'puff piece' terminology
jokes.punchline: The escalating sexual innuendo of 'rough puff' with growling sound effects
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but effective escalation of unintentional innuendo in professional context

jokes.text: He is moving a legless horse.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what a person is doing
jokes.punchline: Bizarre explanation of moving furniture that sounds like abstract art
jokes.explanation: Surreal imagery that's both specific and nonsensical - peak Veep absurdism

jokes.text: I'm taking this really cool class where we sort of debate current issues through movement.
jokes.setup: Catherine trying to impress the interviewer about her studies
jokes.punchline: The pretentious absurdity of 'debating through movement'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Catherine character moment - earnest pretension that sounds completely ridiculous

jokes.text: Looks like I'm masturbating if I got my hands in my pockets.
jokes.setup: Trying to hide Selina's appearance issues for TV
jokes.punchline: Blunt, crude assessment of how the pose looks
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina - no filter, crude but accurate observation about TV staging

jokes.text: Put the blanket over it, or is that too FDR?
jokes.setup: Trying to solve Selina's visual presentation problem
jokes.punchline: Reference to FDR's wheelchair being hidden from the public
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference that's both clever and tasteless - excellent political satire

jokes.text: 'Cause I feel like I fell into a well or something and it's hurting my neck.
jokes.setup: Everyone kneeling down around Selina for the camera setup
jokes.punchline: Her complaint about feeling like she fell into a well
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy about the absurdity of TV production staging

jokes.text: Well, I took my ex down to the river and I tried to smash his head in with a rock and then drown--
jokes.setup: Preparing for questions about the rafting trip
jokes.punchline: Escalating from rafting to attempted murder, cut off mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that reveals Selina's true feelings about Andrew, perfectly interrupted

jokes.text: I'm sorry, am I interrupting a team prayer meeting?
jokes.setup: Walking in on the team huddled around Selina for height adjustment
jokes.punchline: Mistaking the staging for a prayer circle
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy callback to the awkward staging moment

jokes.text: We're just doing a height thing. It was nothing.
jokes.setup: Cody questioning the prayer meeting appearance
jokes.punchline: Gary's awkward, dismissive explanation that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary awkwardness - makes the situation more uncomfortable by trying to fix it

jokes.text: she's wearing a bluish dress like she vomited flowers all over herself
jokes.setup: Describing Janet's outfit for strategic purposes
jokes.punchline: Disgusting but vivid description of floral pattern dress
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disgusting imagery that perfectly captures both the dress and Dan's cruelty

jokes.text: She's kind of a frumpy librarian.
jokes.setup: Continuing the assessment of Janet's appearance
jokes.punchline: Classic put-down that's both specific and dismissive
jokes.explanation: Functional insult but lacks originality - standard political operative cruelty

jokes.text: Just drag her in with the kindness, then turn great white and start taking chunks out of her. She's not much of a meal.
jokes.setup: Describing interview strategy
jokes.punchline: Extended shark metaphor ending with dismissal of Janet as prey
jokes.explanation: Excellent extended metaphor that builds to a perfect punchline dismissal

jokes.text: That's right. I think you've grown since then. Either that or you've gotten shorter.
jokes.setup: Polite greeting between Selina and Janet
jokes.punchline: Selina's attempt at humor that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - trying to be charming but saying something nonsensical

jokes.text: No, it's me. I've got heels on. Well, anyway, I'm so happy you're here.
jokes.setup: Selina's confusing height comment
jokes.punchline: Janet's polite attempt to make sense of it and move on
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Janet trying to navigate Selina's awkwardness

jokes.text: So you found the joint okay.
jokes.setup: Trying to make casual conversation
jokes.punchline: Using slang 'joint' for the Vice President's residence
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Selina's inappropriate casualness in formal setting

jokes.text: The three of them were excellent, so no pressure.
jokes.setup: Janet mentioning this is their fourth vice presidential interview
jokes.punchline: Saying 'no pressure' after explicitly creating pressure
jokes.explanation: Classic passive-aggressive journalism - creating pressure while denying it

jokes.text: We're talking about the building, right? Because I'm original.
jokes.setup: Janet asking about what's original vs. augmented over the years
jokes.punchline: Selina misunderstanding and defensively asserting her own originality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - narcissistic misunderstanding that reveals insecurity

jokes.text: Many of the objay-ects-- objects
jokes.setup: Selina giving a tour, trying to sound sophisticated
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'objects' in an affected way then correcting herself
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe moment - trying too hard to sound cultured and failing

jokes.text: To see spies. If only, right?
jokes.setup: Explaining a telescope/spyglass
jokes.punchline: Joking about seeing spies, unaware of the spy scandal about to hit
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - joking about the exact scandal that's about to destroy her

jokes.text: This is a bell.
jokes.setup: Trying to continue the sophisticated tour after being grilled about the CIA operative
jokes.punchline: Reducing to the most basic possible description of an object
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - two words that perfectly capture Selina's flustered state

jokes.text: This is some old shit by somebody, I don't know who it is.
jokes.setup: Walking through the residence, still trying to give a tour
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown of sophistication into crude dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from 'this is a bell' to complete informational breakdown

jokes.text: I hate this house, to tell you the truth. It's like living in a doll's jail.
jokes.setup: Janet asking if the house is haunted
jokes.punchline: Selina's honest, vivid description of hating the Vice President's residence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, quotable metaphor that perfectly captures the suffocating nature of political life

jokes.text: But that looks like I have a stomachache.
jokes.setup: Crew trying to fix Selina's appearance for camera
jokes.punchline: Her complaint about how the solution looks
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy about the absurdity of TV staging and vanity

jokes.text: Yeah, let me just pull that out of my ass. It's DC. You can find someone willing to open their ass on a Sunday.
jokes.setup: Request for more lighting equipment
jokes.punchline: Crude response escalating to cynical observation about DC corruption
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with pointed political satire about DC's culture of availability

jokes.text: It's DC. You can find someone willing to open their ass on a Sunday.
jokes.setup: Following up on the ass-pulling comment
jokes.punchline: Dark observation about DC's corrupt availability
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about DC corruption, though a bit crude

jokes.text: Sadly, I don't have a lot of time to read fiction.
jokes.setup: Janet asking about a political book
jokes.punchline: Dismissing it as fiction, implying it's inaccurate gossip
jokes.explanation: Sharp political dodge that works as both defense and insult

jokes.text: No, I am hoping to get the box set for Christmas, though.
jokes.setup: Gary asking if she's seen television news before
jokes.punchline: Treating TV news like a DVD collection she can catch up on
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan response that treats journalism like entertainment media

jokes.text: Jesus, you might as well cut out eyeholes and peer through a fucking newspaper. What the fuck are you doing? It's 'Apocalypse Now.'
jokes.setup: Mike trying to be subtle about reading something during the interview
jokes.punchline: Amy's outraged description of his terrible spy technique with movie reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy with perfect movie reference - classic Amy rage

jokes.text: Andrew's fucking us again. He's screwed us in all known dimensions.
jokes.setup: Learning about Andrew's latest scandal
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic description of Andrew's destructive capabilities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with sci-fi imagery that captures the scope of Andrew's damage

jokes.text: He's political poison. He's political--
jokes.setup: Continuing to describe Andrew's toxicity
jokes.punchline: Getting so worked up he can't even finish the thought
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dan's frustration but not particularly original

jokes.text: And then there's one of those winking emoticons at the bottom there.
jokes.setup: Reading Andrew's incriminating email
jokes.punchline: The detail about the emoticon making it even more damning and pathetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that makes Andrew's corruption both more pathetic and more damning

jokes.text: We're not telepathically linked. He can't read my mind.
jokes.setup: Defending herself from Andrew's claims
jokes.punchline: The absurd literal defense against influence
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist defense that actually makes the relationship sound weirder

jokes.text: Ow! Whoa! Okay, my ears are my livelihood. You don't need to--
jokes.setup: Gary trying to fix Janet's microphone too
jokes.punchline: Janet's dramatic declaration about her ears being her livelihood
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Janet's self-importance about her journalism career

jokes.text: When I invited you here to the residence to fuck me, I didn't mean this.
jokes.setup: Selina confronting Andrew about his damaging email
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'fuck' - sexual vs. metaphorically screwing her over
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that reveals their relationship while expressing her fury - quintessential Veep

jokes.text: How easy is it for the Vice President of the United States of America to get some casual sex, hmm? I'm convenient.
jokes.setup: Andrew suggesting they're using each other
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutal assessment of her own sexual situation as VP
jokes.explanation: Dark, honest moment that reveals the personal cost of political power - excellent character work

jokes.text: That's right, I am the Vice President of the United States and I choose now not to fuck you anymore.
jokes.setup: The entire argument about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Selina asserting her power in the crudest possible terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - using her title for petty personal power in the crudest way possible

jokes.text: Oh, Andrew's been veep-fucking? You knew about this?
jokes.setup: Discovering Selina and Andrew's relationship
jokes.punchline: Coining 'veep-fucking' as a term
jokes.explanation: Brilliant neologism that perfectly captures the situation - instantly quotable

jokes.text: We don't have a front door in our attic like in some sort of Dr. Seuss book or something.
jokes.setup: TV crew wanting to film Andrew's entrance
jokes.punchline: Amy's frustrated explanation of why that's architecturally impossible
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy with perfect Dr. Seuss reference for absurd TV demands

jokes.text: Okay, this is a category five shit storm. She's over. She's done. I'm gone.
jokes.setup: Amy realizing the full implications
jokes.punchline: The hurricane metaphor escalating to career disaster
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Amy's panic and self-preservation

jokes.text: Once a week we get together for a gab and a gobble.
jokes.setup: Explaining family dinners to Janet
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual-sounding description of family meals
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre that Selina is oblivious to - classic accidental innuendo

jokes.text: Knock, knock, J Rock is in the-- Whoa, whoa, whoa.
jokes.setup: Jonah barging into the interview
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous entrance getting cut off by the filming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - obnoxious self-promotion interrupted by reality

jokes.text: I give great talking head.
jokes.setup: Jonah offering his services to Janet
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual innuendo while trying to sound professional
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah obliviousness to sexual innuendo while trying to self-promote

jokes.text: Janet, it's like when you loosen a pickle jar and somebody comes by and they just open it. This is the shutdown lid loosener. The pickle jar loosener.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain her role in ending the shutdown
jokes.punchline: Increasingly convoluted pickle jar metaphor that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment showing Selina's desperate attempt to claim credit

jokes.text: I don't need a peeler. Thank you. Yeah, you need a peeler. Actually, I don't.
jokes.setup: Cooking segment with Andrew and Selina
jokes.punchline: Petty argument about vegetable peeling in front of cameras
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing their marriage issues playing out over cooking tools

jokes.text: Well, you probably have so much staff in and out of here-- No, no, no. I know this kitchen like the back of my hand. Oh, that's not it. Okay, I don't know where-- This is maddening.
jokes.setup: Selina claiming to know her kitchen well
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction as she can't find anything
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's competence claims immediately falling apart

jokes.text: I'm not here. Okay, you're not here. I'm so sorry. I'm talking to myself. Where are you, peeler?
jokes.setup: Janet trying to be invisible during filming
jokes.punchline: Selina's increasing desperation while talking to inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Selina's kitchen incompetence into full breakdown

jokes.text: Are you working for the vegetable lobby?
jokes.setup: Catherine enthusiastically talking about loving vegetables
jokes.punchline: Janet's cynical DC assumption about even innocent statements
jokes.explanation: Perfect DC cynicism - assuming even vegetable enthusiasm must be paid promotion

jokes.text: You never told me that you were a vegetarian. I told you three months ago. No, you didn't, Catherine.
jokes.setup: Catherine refusing to eat chicken on camera
jokes.punchline: Mother-daughter argument about basic communication
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Selina's disconnection from her own daughter

jokes.text: You swallowed chicken your entire life. You're gonna do it again today. I swallowed your bullshit my entire life.
jokes.setup: Selina demanding Catherine eat chicken for the cameras
jokes.punchline: Catherine's brutal comeback using the same verb structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural parallel that escalates from food to emotional abuse - Catherine's best line

jokes.text: With all due respect, this is not news. This is the fucking Food Channel you got here. Okay, well, with all due respect, fuck you.
jokes.setup: Amy complaining about the Andrew email story
jokes.punchline: Professional politeness immediately abandoned for direct hostility
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing political niceness breaking down into honest hostility

jokes.text: Although not a great parent, admittedly. Well, actually, he did okay with his own kids. It's when other kids got involved that it got real dicey. But, no, then he did dangle that one from a great height.
jokes.setup: Catherine comparing her parents to Michael Jackson
jokes.punchline: Detailed discussion of Michael Jackson's parenting controversies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation into dark territory, though the Michael Jackson reference feels slightly dated

jokes.text: If you'd just let someone get close enough to fuck some sunshine into you, you'd shut your mouth!
jokes.setup: Amy and Janet's escalating argument
jokes.punchline: Janet's crude psychological assessment disguised as an insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, crude metaphor that's both an insult and amateur psychology - very quotable

jokes.text: Look, I've graphed her performance, okay? Look at this. Look at this nice little graphic. Here's time, here's her fucking career.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to show Amy how badly Selina is doing
jokes.punchline: Dan having actually made a graph of Selina's declining career
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan character moment - treating political careers like business metrics

jokes.text: Amy, when my memaw used to find herself getting snappy, she would sing her favorite song. ♪ Oh, I wish I were in the land of cotton ♪
jokes.setup: Mike trying to calm Amy down with family wisdom
jokes.punchline: His grandmother's 'favorite song' being a Confederate anthem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy revealing Mike's oblivious family racism while trying to be helpful

jokes.text: White water rafting. - Italy. - Disneyland. - Which is it? - No, honey, we didn't ever go to Disneyland. - Oh, no, you didn't take me. Rosa took me with her family. - Who's Rosa? - She was a... - She was... - housekeeper.
jokes.setup: Family trying to agree on vacation memories
jokes.punchline: Revealing Catherine was raised by the housekeeper
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing family dysfunction and neglect

jokes.text: Who's Rosa? She was a... She was... housekeeper. Housekeeper, that's right. You spent a lot of time with your housekeeper?
jokes.setup: Catherine mentioning Rosa took her to Disneyland
jokes.punchline: Parents awkwardly realizing they don't know their own child's relationships
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment revealing how disconnected the parents are from Catherine's life

jokes.text: How about this? How about I lift up my dress, give everyone a big fat shot of my cooch?
jokes.setup: Dan telling Selina to do something big and bold
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude, desperate suggestion for attention
jokes.explanation: Good Selina moment showing her desperation and crude problem-solving approach

jokes.text: Like a medley from 'Okla'-fucking-'homa'?
jokes.setup: Still trying to understand what 'big and bold' means
jokes.punchline: The random musical theater reference
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation showing Selina's panic and confusion

jokes.text: No, no, no. This way, I get a Christmas and a birthday present off of each of them.
jokes.setup: Janet asking if Catherine wants her parents back together
jokes.punchline: Catherine's practical assessment of divorce benefits
jokes.explanation: Good Catherine moment showing her practical, somewhat mercenary approach to family

jokes.text: Calling Tel Aviv now.
jokes.setup: Learning Catherine's boyfriend Rahim is Iranian
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption about international implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that capture DC's paranoid foreign policy mindset

jokes.text: Because in politics, a backbone and a heart are only as good as your ears. And my ears are my livelihood.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to sound presidential and wise
jokes.punchline: Accidentally quoting Janet's earlier line about ears being her livelihood
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Selina unconsciously plagiarizing mid-interview

jokes.text: I spewed out so much bullshit, I'm gonna need a mint. A fucking mint.
jokes.setup: Post-interview assessment of her performance
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude but accurate self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good Selina self-awareness moment with her typical crude honesty

jokes.text: it's like a victory fuck? He better come down.
jokes.setup: Catherine realizing her parents are upstairs together
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact assessment of what's happening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Catherine moment - clinical assessment of her parents' relationship dynamics

tmdb_overview: After being prepped for a “puff piece” interview at the VP residence, Selina is thrown by Janet Ryland’s “gotcha” questions.
insight: Crisis escalation drives 66 jokes as Meyer stumbles through her first major test.
